name: The Chinquapin School
about: Chinquapin Preparatory School is a nonprofit, independent college-preparatory school serving low-income youth, primarily minority students, from the Greater Houston area. Founded in 1969 by Robert and Maxine Moore, it sits on a rural campus in Highlands, about 20 miles east of downtown Houston. Five-day weekly boarding is offered to students in grades 7-12, and nearly all students receive up to a 97% tuition scholarship.
accreditation: Texas Alliance of Accredited Private Schools
notable_alumni: Jarvis Johnson (Texas House of Representatives), Nhial "Simon" Malia (Lost Boy of Sudan)
financial_aid_notes: Students benefit from up to a 97% tuition scholarship; the school primarily serves low-income families and operates on a need-based scholarship model funded through donations and endowment.
athletic_conference: Texas Association of Private and Parochial Schools (TAPPS), Division 2A

name: Hockaday School
about: The Hockaday School is an independent, secular college-preparatory day school for girls in grades PK-12, located on a 100-acre campus in North Dallas. Founded in 1913 by Ela Hockaday, it is one of the largest independent girls' schools in the United States. The school phased out its historic boarding program by 2025 and now operates exclusively as a day school.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend selective colleges nationwide; since 2010 five Hockaday graduates have become Rhodes Scholars at the University of Oxford.
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Southwest (ISAS)
notable_alumni: Lisa Loeb (singer-songwriter), Patricia Richardson (actress), Victoria Clark (Tony Award-winning actress), Farrah Forke (actress)
financial_aid_notes: Hockaday awards more than $3 million in financial aid each year to 12% of its families.

name: Luther Preparatory School
about: Luther Preparatory School is a coeducational residential secondary school in Watertown, Wisconsin, owned and operated by the Wisconsin Evangelical Lutheran Synod (WELS). Established in 1865, it serves grades 9-12 on a 40-acre campus and prepares students for further training as pastors and teachers in the WELS. About three-quarters of its roughly 392 students live in the dormitories.
financial_aid_notes: Every LPS student is automatically subsidized by the synod, which covers a large portion of the cost of education. The school also distributes need-based aid from the LPS Student Assistance Fund, ranging from $200 to $4,800, and participates in Wisconsin parental choice programs.

name: St. John's Northwestern Military Academy
about: St. John's Northwestern Academy (formerly St. John's Northwestern Military Academy) is a coeducational independent boarding and day school in Delafield, Wisconsin, about 30 miles west of Milwaukee. Founded in 1884, it serves grades 6-12 plus a post-graduate year and offers both a JROTC-based military leadership track and a non-military Leadership Academy. The school enrolls a substantial international population and offers honors, AP, and college-credit coursework.
notable_alumni: Spencer Tracy (actor), Daniel Gerber (Gerber founder), Ty Warner (Beanie Babies/Ty Inc.), Martin Torrijos (President of Panama), George F. Kennan, Brandin Podziemski (NBA, Golden State Warriors)
financial_aid_notes: Financial aid and scholarships are available to qualifying families.

name: St. Lawrence Seminary High School
about: St. Lawrence Seminary High School is a Roman Catholic, all-male, seven-day residential college-preparatory school in Mount Calvary, Wisconsin, operated by the Province of St. Joseph of the Capuchin Order. Founded in 1860, it sits on an 80-acre hilltop campus and serves about 170 students in grades 9-12. Although the true cost of attendance is roughly $53,000, benefactor support keeps the charge to families at about $18,000 per year.
religious_affiliation: Roman Catholic (Capuchin Franciscan / Order of Friars Minor Capuchin)
accreditation: North Central Association of Colleges and Schools
notable_alumni: Bishop Fabian Bruskewitz, George N. Shuster (author and Hunter College president)
financial_aid_notes: Actual cost is about $53,000 per year but families are charged about $18,000 thanks to benefactor support; about 89.7% of domestic students receive some form of need-based financial aid.
head_of_school: Fr. Zoy Garibay, OFMCap (Rector); David Bartel (Director of School)

name: Wayland Academy
about: Wayland Academy is a coeducational, independent college-preparatory boarding and day school in Beaver Dam, Wisconsin, about 90 minutes from Milwaukee. Founded in 1855 and originally Baptist-affiliated, it now operates as a secular school on a 55-acre campus serving about 200 students in grades 9-12. International students make up roughly 20-25% of the student body, which represents many states and over 30 countries.
accreditation: ISACS (Independent Schools Association of the Central States); NCA CASI
notable_alumni: Ric Flair (professional wrestler), Addie Joss (Baseball Hall of Fame), Zona Gale (Pulitzer Prize-winning author)
financial_aid_notes: Wayland offered over $6,000,000 in tuition assistance through financial aid and scholarships last year; merit and endowed scholarships range from $2,500 to $25,000. Financial aid applications are processed through Clarity.
